name: San Francisco Fudge Foggies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unsalted butter, cut into 1/3 cups
recipeInstructions:
1. Position a rack in the center of the oven and preheat to 375°F.
2. Line a 9x13 inch baking pan with double thickness of aluminum foil extending 2 inches beyond the sides of the pan. Butter the bottom and sides of the foil-lined pan.
3. In the top of a double boiler set over hot, not simmering water, melt chocolate, butter, and dissolved coffee, stirring frequently until smooth.
4. Cool the mixture, stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
5. In a large bowl, beat eggs at high speed for 30 seconds, or until foamy.
6. Gradually add sugar and continue to beat for 2 minutes, or until the mixture is very light and fluffy.
7. Reduce mixer speed to low and gradually beat in chocolate mixture until just blended.
8. Stir in flour using a wooden spoon. Stir in walnuts. Do not overbeat the mixture.
9. Transfer batter to prepared pan. Bake for 28-30 minutes, or until foggies are set around the edges. They will remain moist in the center.
10. Cool foggies in the pan on a wire rack for 30 minutes. Cover pan tightly with aluminum foil and refrigerate overnight or for at least 6 hours.
11. Remove top foil. Run a sharp knife around the edge of the foggies. Using two ends of the foil as handles, lift foggies onto a large plate and peel off foil. Invert foggies again onto a smooth surface and cut into 16 rectangles.
12. Makes 16 foggies.
